key_insight: CADMM-Prox adds a proximal outer-level update to Consensus ADMM to stabilize distributed optimization for non-smooth, non-convex problems and converge to a neighborhood of a Clarke stationary point.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical distributed optimization tool for machine learning, control, and signal-processing workflows where sparsity and non-convex objectives make standard methods unstable.
method: Combine classical Consensus ADMM with a bi-level structure.
Introduce a sufficiently large proximal term tied to an outer-level variable.
Assume local objectives are semi-convex to obtain global neighborhood convergence.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on phase retrieval, where CADMM-Prox shows more stable convergence than the baseline algorithm.
watch_item: The guarantee is convergence only to a neighborhood of a Clarke stationary point, so solution accuracy may depend on proximal tuning and problem conditioning.
paper_title: CADMM-Prox: A Bi-level Consensus ADMM for Non-smooth Non-convex Distributed Consensus Optimization
